2604.14970.title: Explain the Flag: Contextualizing Hate Speech Beyond Censorship
2604.14970.paper_summary: Hate, derogatory, and offensive speech remains a persistent challenge in online platforms and public discourse. While automated detection systems are widely used, most focus on censorship or removal, raising concerns for transparency and freedom of expression, and limiting opportunities to explain why content is harmful. To address these issues, explanatory approaches have emerged as a promising solution, aiming to make hate speech detection more transparent, accountable, and informative. In this paper, we present a hybrid approach that combines Large Language Models (LLMs) with three newly created and curated vocabularies to detect and explain hate speech in English, French, and Greek. Our system captures both inherently derogatory expressions tied to identity characteristics and direct group-targeted content through two complementary pipelines: one that detects and disambiguates problematic terms using the curated vocabularies, and one that leverages LLMs as context-aware evaluators of group-targeting content. The outputs are fused into grounded explanations that clarify why content is flagged. Human evaluation shows that our hybrid approach is accurate, with high-quality explanations, outperforming LLM-only baselines.
2604.14970.paper_summary_zh: 仇恨、貶義和冒犯性言論在網路平台和公共話語中仍然是一個持續的挑戰。雖然自動檢測系統被廣泛使用，但大多數系統專注於審查或移除，這引發了對透明度和言論自由的擔憂，並限制了解釋內容為何有害的機會。為了解決這些問題，解釋性方法已經出現，作為一種有前景的解決方案，旨在使仇恨言論檢測更加透明、負責和具有信息性。在本文中，我們提出了一種混合方法，將大型語言模型（LLMs）與三個新創建和策劃的詞彙結合起來，以檢測和解釋英語、法語和希臘語中的仇恨言論。 我們的系統通過兩個互補的流程捕捉與身份特徵相關的固有貶義表達和直接針對群體的內容：一個使用策劃的詞彙來檢測和消歧義問題術語，另一個利用LLMs作為對群體針對內容的上下文感知評估者。這些輸出融合成具體的解釋，澄清為何內容被標記。人類評估顯示，我們的混合方法準確，解釋質量高，超越了僅使用LLM的基準。
2604.14970.author: Jason Liartis et.al.
2604.14970.authors: Jason Liartis, Eirini Kaldeli, Lambrini Gyftokosta, Eleftherios Chelioudakis, Orfeas Menis Mastromichalakis
